Refer to the Other Sources section of the Metadata to learn additional sources used for the data sheets

For the February 1952 elections, candidate spellings and symbols follow the 1952 Federation of Malaya Gazette (Selangor), 28th February 1952. For the December 1952 elections, canddiate spellings and symbols follow the 1952 Federation of Malaya Gazette (Selangor), 14th November 1952. Even though candidates affiliated with political parties were allowed to contest, no party logos were used as candidate symbols. No information about candidates losing deposits. No turnout data was provided. TindakMalaysia Network Services PLT is not responsible for any errors or omissions arising from the original data or its derivatives.

column1: A spoiled ballot paper means a ballot paper which, on polling day has not been deposited in the ballot box, but has been found by the presiding officer to be spoiled or improperly printed or which has been handed by the presiding officer to an elector to cast his vote, and (a) has been spoiled in marking by the elector, and (b) has been handed back to the presiding officer and exchanged for another

A rejected ballot paper means a ballot paper which has been handed by the presiding officer to an elector to cast his vote but which, at the close of the poll, has been found in the ballot box unmarked or so improperly marked that it cannot be counted.
